us.json.notes: US fixedLine and mobile patterns are byte-identical upstream (NANP doesn't distinguish them by number range), so fixedLine's pattern was used for the general validation pattern. possibleLengths uses only the national length (10); the localOnly length (7, e.g. UAN-style 310-xxxx numbers) was intentionally dropped since DialSense only parses full E.164 numbers, where a local-only length is never valid.

ca.json.notes: Added alongside us.json to prove calling code 1 (NANP) can resolve to more than one region: US and Canada share calling code 1 but have disjoint area-code allocations, so parse() tries each region sharing a matched calling code and returns whichever one's pattern actually validates the number. Same localOnly-length-dropped rationale as us.json.
